content: Kelly Electric LLC. Licensed master electrician, PA license PA-ME-019472, renewed 2025. Residential and small commercial. Serving Bucks, Montgomery, and Delaware counties. Call for a quote.
excerpt: Licensed master electrician serving Bucks, Montgomery, and Delaware counties.
title: About Kelly Electric

content: Old 100-amp panels run short on circuits. A 200-amp upgrade gives you room for AC, EV charging, and a generator. Permit and inspection included. Most upgrades done in a day.
excerpt: When and why to upgrade to a 200-amp service.
title: Time for a 200-amp panel upgrade?

content: If your bathroom and kitchen outlets don't trip-test, they may not be GFCI protected. Code has required them for years. Cheap fix, real safety gain.
excerpt: GFCI outlets in older homes are worth the small fix.
title: GFCI outlets in older homes

content: Level 2 EV charger needs a dedicated 240-volt circuit. I size the wire to the charger and run it clean to the garage. Permit pulled where required.
excerpt: What a Level 2 EV charger install involves.
title: Installing a home EV charger

content: A standby generator needs a transfer switch wired to your panel. I handle the electrical side and coordinate with your gas or propane installer. No backfeeding, ever.
excerpt: Wiring a generator in safely with a transfer switch.
title: Generator hookups done right

content: Warm outlets, flickering lights, breakers that trip often. These are warnings, not quirks. Get them looked at before they become a fire.
excerpt: Signs your wiring needs attention.
title: Five warning signs in your wiring

content: Hardwired smoke detectors with battery backup are required in most homes. If yours are 10 years old, replace them. I can interconnect them so all sound together.
excerpt: Replace old smoke detectors and interconnect them.
title: Smoke detectors: when to replace

content: A subpanel in the garage or basement saves long runs and gives you dedicated circuits for shop tools or a freezer. Sized to your load.
excerpt: When a subpanel makes sense.
title: Adding a subpanel

content: Power strips are not extension cords. Don't daisy-chain them. For permanent needs, add an outlet. It's safer and cheaper over time.
excerpt: Stop overloading power strips.
title: Power strips and overloaded outlets

content: I cover Bucks, Montgomery, and Delaware counties out of Fox Chase, Philadelphia. Booking runs four to six weeks for larger jobs. Call early for fall and winter work.
excerpt: Service area and current booking lead time.
title: Service area and scheduling

content: Outdoor outlets must be weather-resistant with in-use covers and GFCI protection. For holiday lights, use timers and don't overload the circuit.
excerpt: Outdoor outlets and holiday lighting safety.
title: Outdoor outlets and holiday lights

content: Aluminum branch wiring from the 1960s and 70s needs proper connectors at every device. If your home has it, get it inspected. It's a known fire risk done wrong.
excerpt: Aluminum wiring needs the right connections.
title: Aluminum wiring in older homes

content: A portable generator needs a proper inlet and interlock, not a suicide cord. I install inlet boxes and interlock kits so you can power circuits safely during an outage.
excerpt: Portable generator inlets and interlocks.
title: Portable generators: inlet and interlock

content: Whole-house surge protectors mount in the panel and protect everything downstream. Worth it for homes with electronics and HVAC. Quick add during a panel job.
excerpt: Why a whole-house surge protector pays off.
title: Whole-house surge protection

content: Knob-and-tube wiring is past its life. Insurers often won't cover it. I rewire room by room with minimal drywall damage, permitted and inspected.
excerpt: Replacing knob-and-tube wiring.
title: Knob-and-tube: time to rewire

content: Every panel breaker should be labeled. If yours aren't, I map and label the whole panel during any service call. Saves time in an emergency.
excerpt: Label your panel for safety and convenience.
title: Label your electrical panel

content: Kitchen remodels need dedicated circuits for the range, dishwasher, microwave, and counter outlets. I rough-in to code and coordinate with your contractor.
excerpt: Electrical for a kitchen remodel.
title: Wiring for a kitchen remodel

content: Extension cords are for temporary use only. Run under rugs or through walls, they overheat. If you need permanent power, let me add an outlet.
excerpt: Extension cords are temporary, not permanent.
title: Extension cords: temporary only

content: EV charger rebates change year to year. Check with your utility before the install. I provide the load calculation and documentation most programs ask for.
excerpt: EV charger rebates and the paperwork you need.
title: EV charger rebates and load calcs

content: Recessed lighting brightens a room without taking floor space. I lay out spacing, cut clean, and wire to a dimmer. Draft listing while I gather photos.
excerpt: Recessed lighting layout and install.
title: Recessed lighting done clean
